sanskrit: कामक्रोधवियुक्तानां यतीनां यतचेतसाम् । अभितो ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं वर्तते विदितात्मनाम् ॥
hindiTranslation: कामक्रोधसे सर्वथा रहित, जीते हुए मनवाले और स्वरूपका साक्षात्कार किये हुए सांख्ययोगियोंके लिये दोनों ओरसेशरीरके रहते हुए अथवा शरीर छूटनेके बाद निर्वाण ब्रह्म परिपूर्ण है ॥
englishTranslation: For those free from desire and anger, with disciplined minds and self-knowledge, freedom in the supreme reality is near on both sides.
essence: Freedom comes when desire and anger no longer govern the mind.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: When desire and anger no longer control the mind, and a person knows the true self, inner freedom is close whether alive or after death.
simpleInsight: There is relief in seeing that freedom begins with inner mastery, not with changing the world.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna stands frozen while Krishna explains the state of one who has gone beyond craving and rage. This verse continues the teaching on inner release: the person who knows the true self can rest in freedom even before the body falls.
modernRelevance: You read the message that set you off, and the reply is already forming. This verse asks you to notice the heat before it becomes action, because freedom starts there.
translationLiteral: काम-क्रोध-वियुक्तानाम् (for those free from desire and anger) / यतीनाम् (for disciplined renouncers) / यत-चेतसाम् (for those with controlled minds) / अभितः (on both sides) / ब्रह्म-निर्वाणम् (freedom in the supreme reality) / वर्तते (is present) / विदित-आत्मनाम् (for those who know the true self)
